more.title: The nicotine Wild West: How South Africa birthed the next generation of nicotine addicts
more.description: Somewhere around 2012, electronic cigarettes began popping up in shops across the country. It was a nicotine-infused Wild West: an open market for slick new delivery devices which arrived with a lot of hype and zero regulation. Apart from complying with import rules, there was no specific limit on advertising like with tobacco, no legal standard on […]

more.title: Congo files ICJ case against Rwanda over role in conflict
more.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has filed a case against Rwanda at the International Court of Justice over its role in the long-running conflict in the east, the government said on Friday. In a statement, Congo accused Rwanda of breaching international conventions, including those on the prevention of genocide, racial discrimination and torture. Congo said Rwanda […]

more.title: Holomisa blames blurred lines for collapse of SA’s municipalities
more.description: Holomisa said once politicians begin deciding who gets contracts, who gets appointed and who benefits from municipal resources, service delivery becomes secondary and communities suffer

more.title: Foreigners at Durban drive-in site to be moved to Limpopo processing centre

more.title: Toyota South Africa – TOUGHER THAN EVER: TOYOTA LAUNCHES THE NEXT CHAPTER OF HILUX
more.description: For nearly six decades, the Toyota Hilux has been far more than a vehicle. It has been a trusted business partner, a dependable workhorse, an adventure companion and, for many South Africans, a symbol of resilience. From remote farms and construction sites to bustling city streets and cross-country expeditions, Hilux has earned its reputation through [...]

more.title: Sanlam Benchmark: South Africans are waiting too long to plan for retirement
more.description: Are South Africans waiting too long to plan for retirement? The latest Sanlam Benchmark Survey reveals alarming trends in retirement readiness, highlighting the need for early financial planning.

more.title: Bloemfontein Anti-Illegal Immigration March Highlights Border Control Demands Despite Low Turnout
more.description: BLOEMFONTEIN, FREE STATE — A recent Bloemfontein anti-illegal immigration march drew a small crowd, but organizers maintain that the low turnout does not diminish the urgent concerns surrounding undocumented foreign nationals and border security in South Africa. The peaceful demonstration, organized by the Forum for Service Delivery, saw only a handful of participants. However, the [...]

more.title: REVEALED: Who Bafana will face if they beat Canada at the World Cup
South Africa’s commitment to trade investment and solidarity
more.description: South Africa’s founding president and global icon, Nelson Mandela, dreamt of a united Africa that would overcome its shared challenges and unlock its immense potential through collective action. He famously said: “I dream of the realisation of the unity of Africa, whereby its leaders combine in their efforts to solve the problems of this continent.” [...]

more.title: South Africa Deportation and Repatriation Operations Expand Amid New Migration Strategy
more.description: South Africa deportation and repatriation operations targeting undocumented foreign nationals are accelerating significantly, marking a major milestone in the government’s newly implemented five-point Comprehensive Approach for Migration Management. Unveiled earlier this month by President Cyril Ramaphosa, the strategic framework relies on lawful, coordinated, and constitutionally sound mechanisms to manage migration. According to Mmamoloko Kubayi, Minister [...]

more.title: Paul Mashatile Urges Mindray to Drive Medical Technology Investment in South Africa
more.description: South African Deputy President Paul Mashatile has formally invited Chinese healthcare manufacturer Mindray to accelerate medical technology investment in South Africa, emphasizing new opportunities in local assembly, manufacturing, and artificial intelligence integration. During a working visit to the People’s Republic of China on Thursday, Mashatile toured the facilities of Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics. The guided tour [...]

more.title: Armed Robbery Suspects Apprehended After High-Speed Pretoria R21 Highway Chase
more.description: GAUTENG — A massive multi-agency operation culminated in the apprehension of three armed robbery suspects following a dramatic, high-speed Pretoria R21 highway chase on Thursday afternoon. The tactical takedown resulted in the recovery of an illegal weapon and left two individuals hospitalized. The joint manhunt was triggered by actionable intelligence identifying a silver Kia Rio [...]
